2606.13603.title: Beyond the Commitment Boundary: Probing Epiphenomenal Chain-of-Thought in Large Reasoning Models
2606.13603.paper_summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning is the dominant paradigm for inference-time scaling in language models, yet the causal influence of individual steps on the final answer poorly understood. We estimate each step's causal importance via early exit and use this measure to study how answers form across the reasoning traces of several model families. Across diverse tasks, we find that reasoning typically crosses a \emph{commitment boundary} -- a sharp transition from transient intermediate guesses to a stable, high-confidence answer. This transition often happens in a single step, well before the model's reasoning block ends, and is followed by \emph{epiphenomenal} CoT steps that leave the final answer probability unaltered. Using attention probes, we show that answer-formation stages can be linearly decoded from intermediate reasoning steps with high accuracy and generalize robustly to unseen reasoning tasks. We exploit this signal to early-exit reasoning blocks at the commitment boundary, reducing the length of CoTs up to 55\% on average with negligible impact on model performance.
2606.13603.paper_summary_zh: 鏈式思考（CoT）推理是語言模型推理時擴展的主導範式，但各個步驟對最終答案的因果影響尚不清楚。<lb/>我們通過早期退出來估計每個步驟的因果重要性，並利用這一度量來研究幾個模型家族的推理軌跡中答案的形成。<lb/>在各種任務中，我們發現推理通常會跨越一個\emph{承諾邊界}——從瞬時的中間猜測到穩定、高信心答案的急劇過渡。<lb/>這一過渡通常在單一步驟中發生，遠在模型的推理塊結束之前，隨後出現\emph{附帶性}的CoT步驟，這些步驟不會改變最終答案的概率。<lb/>通過注意力探測，我們顯示答案形成階段可以從中間推理步驟中以高準確率線性解碼，並能穩健地泛化到未見的推理任務。<lb/>我們利用這一信號在承諾邊界處提前退出推理塊，平均將CoT的長度減少了55\%，對模型性能的影響微乎其微。
2606.13603.author: Daniel Scalena et.al.
2606.13603.authors: Daniel Scalena, Sara Candussio, Luca Bortolussi, Elisabetta Fersini, Malvina Nissim, Gabriele Sarti
